blocks.text: Bramblewood Legal PLLC
Jennifer Chavez, General Manager
Miles, Alexander and Mccoy
Re: Engagement for Lease Dispute Advisory
Dear Ms. Chavez:

This letter confirms that Bramblewood Legal PLLC has been engaged by {{fact:f:E-2018-001.client}} to advise on the lease dispute now pending with respect to the premises occupied by Miles, Alexander and Mccoy. We are grateful for the confidence this engagement reflects, and we set out below, as is our practice, exactly what we will do, who will do it, and on what terms.

Our scope is advisory. We will review the lease, its amendments, and the correspondence exchanged between the parties; examine the parcel records and recorded filings bearing on the disputed provisions; assess the positions available to you; and prepare such notices, responses, and settlement documentation as the matter requires. Work beyond this scope, including any court proceeding, will be undertaken only under a written amendment to this letter signed by both of us. An engagement that states its limits plainly at the outset tends to keep them.

Nicholas Cox, Senior Attorney, will lead the matter. Ashley Reese, Attorney, will conduct the analysis and prepare the periodic status reports to your office. Brendan Walls, Paralegal, will maintain the matter file and the record of filings. Consistent with the firm's standing access rules, the client folder for this matter will be readable by this team and by me, and by no one else at the firm.

Our fee for the engagement is a fixed fee of {{fact:f:E-2018-001.fee}}, covering the scope described above. Recording charges and other third-party costs are not included and will be billed to you as incurred, with supporting documentation. Work will commence on {{fact:f:E-2018-001.start}}, and we expect the engagement to run approximately five months.

If these terms match your understanding, please sign where indicated below and return one executed copy to my attention. Questions at any point in the matter are welcome; you will find we answer them plainly. We look forward to the work.
Memorandum
To: Ashley Reese; Brendan Walls
From: Nicholas Cox
Re: Kickoff, Lease Dispute Advisory for Miles, Alexander and Mccoy

The engagement letter is signed. Our client is {{fact:f:E-2018-001.client}}. Our contact is Jennifer Chavez, General Manager. The engagement runs from {{fact:f:E-2018-001.start}} for roughly five months. The objective is simple to state: put the client in the strongest defensible position on the disputed lease provisions before the parties start talking numbers. The record comes first. Every parcel and every filing gets listed before anyone gets clever.

items: Lease record. Assemble the lease, all amendments, estoppels, and the party correspondence. Brendan Walls owns this.
Title and filings. Confirm the parcel descriptions and pull every recorded instrument that touches the premises. I own this.
Position analysis. Draft the assessment of the disputed provisions and the options open to the client. Ashley Reese owns this.
Client reporting. Status reports to Jennifer Chavez in a form her office can forward upward without a phone call. Ashley Reese owns this.

blocks.text: Next steps. Brendan circulates the document inventory by the end of this week. Ashley schedules the first working session with the client. I will report on the parcel records when the review is done, not before. The matter folder is restricted to this team and the Managing Partner; keep it that way.
Working Session Minutes: Lease Dispute Advisory for Miles, Alexander and Mccoy
Prepared by Brendan Walls, Paralegal. Minutes taken in the room and circulated to all attendees the same day.

items: Nicholas Cox, Senior Attorney, Bramblewood Legal PLLC
Ashley Reese, Attorney, Bramblewood Legal PLLC
Brendan Walls, Paralegal, Bramblewood Legal PLLC (minutes)
Jennifer Chavez, General Manager, Miles, Alexander and Mccoy

blocks.text: Nicholas Cox opened with the state of the record. The parcel and filing review is complete. The recorded instruments were walked through one by one, and no filing was identified that undercuts the client's reading of the disputed provisions. Ashley Reese then presented the draft position analysis and took comments in the room.

Jennifer Chavez asked that the next status report state the open items plainly enough that the general counsel of {{fact:f:E-2018-001.client}} can read it without a covering call. Ashley Reese agreed to that format. One exhibit is still missing from the correspondence file; the gap is recorded here so it is not forgotten, and it is assigned below.

rows: Obtain the missing correspondence exhibit and complete the matter file
Revise the position analysis to reflect comments taken in session
Prepare the next status report in the format requested by the client
Set the agenda and date for the next working session

blocks.text: Status Report: Lease Dispute Advisory for Miles, Alexander and Mccoy
Prepared by Nicholas Cox, Senior Attorney, Bramblewood Legal PLLC, for the executive office of {{fact:f:E-2018-001.client}}.
Status summary

The matter is on schedule and within budget. The engagement remains within the fixed fee of {{fact:f:E-2018-001.fee}} set in the engagement letter; no change to that figure is proposed or expected. The documentary record is complete, the position analysis is settled, and the remaining work is drafting.
Accomplishments this period

items: Completed the review of parcel records and recorded filings; no instrument was found that weakens the client's reading of the disputed provisions.
Ashley Reese finalized the position analysis, incorporating comments taken at the working session with the client.
Brendan Walls closed the last gap in the correspondence file; the matter record is now complete.

blocks.text: Upcoming work

items: Draft the settlement documentation reflecting the settled position analysis.
Prepare a short response plan for each answer the counterparty may plausibly give.
Continue reporting in this format through the close of the engagement.

blocks.text: Risks

Two risks are worth stating. First, the counterparty controls its own timetable; a slow response would push the closing weeks of the engagement, though not the fee. Second, any position the counterparty presents as standard language will be tested against the record before it is accepted. Neither risk requires action from your office at this time. Questions on this report may be directed to me or to Ashley Reese.
